raw_consumer_statement:
On XX/XX/year>, I enrolled in an online coaching program ( XXXX XXXX XXXX XXXX ) and was charged {$7000.00} on my Citi Mastercard. 
Within an hour of the transaction on the same day ( XX/XX/year> ), I submitted a written cancellation request to the merchant after discovering a pricing discrepancy between publicly referenced pricing ( approximately {$5000.00} ) and the {$7000.00} amount charged. 
At the time of cancellation, I had only created a login profile as part of onboarding and had not accessed any course materials, attended coaching calls, downloaded resources, or received any services. 
The merchant responded stating that the program was non-refundable, despite my immediate cancellation and lack of service usage. 
I then filed a dispute with Citi shortly after the transaction, providing documentation of : Same-day cancellation Pricing discrepancy No services received Citi issued a provisional credit during the investigation. 
On XX/XX/year>, Citi denied my dispute and reversed the provisional credit, stating they were unable to determine that a billing error occurred. 
However, this determination did not properly consider that : The transaction was cancelled within hours, no services were rendered or used, the merchant revoked access immediately after I filed the dispute. 
There was a material pricing discrepancy that directly impacted my decision to cancel immediately. 
I am requesting that Citis handling of this dispute be reviewed, as I believe this qualifies as a valid dispute for services not received and cancellation prior to delivery, and was not properly evaluated.
